Dietitian
Licensed Nutritionist (LN) / Licensed Dietitian Guide

Issued by the South Dakota Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners (SDBMOE) under the Department of Health. Data verified for 2026.

Total Initial Fee
$70
$70 initial application fee
Renewal
$100
Every 2 years (expires May 31 of odd years)
Processing Time
3 to 4 Weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
Up to 2 Years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

South Dakota Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners (SDBMOE)

Mailing Address

101 N. Main Ave., Suite 301
Sioux Falls, SD 57104

Physical Address

101 N. Main Ave., Suite 301

Phone & Fax

Phone: (605) 367-7781

Fax: (605) 367-7786

Email

sdbmoe@state.sd.us

Credential Categories

South Dakota Dietitian License Types

Licensed Nutritionist / Dietitian

The credential to practice dietetics in South Dakota, regulated by the Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners. The state law primarily licenses under the 'Nutritionist' title but requires qualifications identical to dietetics.

Application Process

How to Apply for a South Dakota Licensed Nutritionist (LN) / Licensed Dietitian

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,200 hours of board-approved supervised practice through a dietetic internship.

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD status satisfies the state's exam requirement.

Submit the Application via SDBMOE

Apply online through the South Dakota Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners portal and pay the $70 application fee.

Provide Verification Documents

Submit a copy of your active CDR registration to verify that you meet the educational, examination, and supervised practice requirements.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official Online ApplicationApplication submitted via the SDBMOE portal.
  • CDR Registration VerificationProof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration. Usually submitted directly or verified online by the Board.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

South Dakota regulations require passing the national examination before full licensure. Check directly with the SDBMOE regarding temporary permits while awaiting examination results.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Applicants licensed in another state may be granted reciprocity if they hold an active license in good standing and can verify their Commission on Dietetic Registration status, demonstrating compliance with South Dakota's requirements.

Frequently Asked Questions

South Dakota Dietitian FAQ

How much does a South Dakota dietitian license cost?

The initial license application fee is $70.

When does the South Dakota license need to be renewed?

South Dakota dietitian licenses expire biennially on May 31st of odd-numbered years. The renewal fee is $100.

Why does South Dakota use the 'Nutritionist' title for Dietitians?

South Dakota codified its practice act using the term 'Licensed Nutritionist' for professionals providing medical nutrition therapy, but the requirements parallel those for Registered Dietitians nationally.
